zh.Hingham, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Briti: 布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼辛加姆
zh.lifeSummary: 當Martha Ward於1581年出生在英國肯特爾哈姆時，她的父親Thomas Ward41歲，母親Parnell Bowles37歲。她於大約1602年與William Lobdell在英國英格蘭素可海斯廷斯結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1641年3月在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼辛加姆享年60歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Martha Ward于1581年出生在英国肯特爾哈姆时，她的父亲Thomas Ward41岁，母亲Parnell Bowles37岁。她于大约1602年与William Lobdell在英国英格兰素可海斯廷斯结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。她去世于1641年3月在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼辛加姆享年60岁。

nameMeanings.name: Ward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a watchman or guard, from Middle English ward ‘watchman, guard’ (Old English weard, used as both an agent noun and an abstract noun).
English: occupational name from Middle English warde ‘armed guard’ (Old English weard ‘watching, guarding’), with the same meaning as 1 above.

Irish: shortened form of McWard, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac an Bhaird ‘son of the poet’. The surname occurs throughout Ireland, where three different branches of the family are known as professional poets.

variants.name: Warshawsky
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Meyer, Ari, Hyman.

Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from the city of Warsaw (Polish Warszawa), which became the capital of Poland at the end of the 16th century, after the destruction of Kraków by fire.
Americanized form of Polish Warszawski, a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Wardman
meanings: English (Yorkshire): occupational name from Middle English wardman, either in the general sense ‘watchman, guard’ (compare Ward ) or in the administrative sense ‘representative of a ward within a borough’.

variants.name: Warde
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Cormac, Donal.

nameMeanings.name: Martha
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, of Aramaic rather than Hebrew origin, meaning ‘lady’. It was borne by the sister of Lazarus and Mary of Bethany (John 11:1). According to Luke 10:38, when Jesus visited the house of Mary and Martha, Mary sat at his feet, listening to him, while Martha ‘was cumbered about much serving’, so that she complained to Jesus, ‘Lord, dost thou not care that my sister hath left me to serve alone?’ For this reason, the name Martha has always been associated with hard domestic work, as opposed to the contemplative life.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Martha Ward was born in 1581, in Chilham, Kent, England, her father, Thomas Ward, was 41 and her mother, Parnell Bowles, was 37. She married William Lobdell about 1602, in Hastings, Sussex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died in March 1641, in Hingham, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 60.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Martha Ward est née en 1581, à Chilham, Kent, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Ward, avait 41 ans et sa mère, Parnell Bowles, avait 37 ans. Elle a épousé William Lobdell environ 1602, à Hastings, Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle est décédée en mars 1641, à Hingham, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 60 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Martha Wardが1581年にイギリスケントチルハムで生まれた時，父Thomas Wardは41歳，母Parnell Bowlesは37歳であった。約1602年にイギリスイギリススセックスハスティングズでWilliam Lobdellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼女は1641年3月にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーヒンガムで60歳で死去した。

ko.Hingham, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Briti: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 잉함
ko.lifeSummary: Martha Ward(이)가 1581년 영국 켄트 치함에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Ward(은)는 41세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Parnell Bowles(은)는 37세였다. 그녀는 대략 1602년 영국 잉글랜드 수세스 하스틴그스에서 William Lobdell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1641년 3월 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 잉함에서 60세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.Hingham, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Briti: хингхам, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Martha Ward родилась в 1581 г. в местности чилхам, кэнт, Англия, её отцу по имени Thomas Ward было 41 год, а матери, которую звали Parnell Bowles, 37. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Lobdell около 1602 г. в местности хастингс, суссекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла марта 1641 г. в местности хингхам, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 60 лет.
